title: China-to-Kazakhstan EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Supply and China GB/T 18487 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Kazakhstan uses 50 Hz power systems, so Chinese EV chargers designed for China are usually frequency-compatible at a basic rating level. The remaining issue is not frequency alone but site voltage, phase configuration, earthing system, power quality, thermal derating, cold-weather operation, surge environment, and local protection coordination.
target_standards: Kazakhstan electricity supply and grid connection technical conditions
IEC 61851 series where specified
Applicable GOST / ST RK electrical installation and power-quality requirements
Project-specific cold-climate, enclosure, and environmental specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China GB/T 18487.1-2023 evidence typically supports 50 Hz operation and charger control behavior. GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, and GB/T 20234.3-2023 connector evidence and GB/T 27930-2023 DC communication evidence may also be included, but they must be mapped to Kazakhstan site conditions and any IEC 61851 / IEC 62196 requirements.
china_standards: China charger environmental and power-quality test reports
rows.gap: Document 50 Hz nameplate suitability, accepted input-voltage range, three-phase and single-phase variants, neutral and earthing assumptions, derating curves, heater or cooling behavior, IP / IK ratings, surge protection, residual-current protection, and low-temperature startup behavior.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by site technical conditions, utility approval, electrical installation rules, tender, or project specification; otherwise a core technical acceptance check
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese product safety, GB/T charger scope, and customer requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Shared 50 Hz frequency reduces one compatibility risk, but it does not close Kazakhstan voltage, earthing, power-quality, environmental, connector, or grid-approval gaps.

rows.target_requirement: Kazakhstan EV charging deployments may specify IEC 62196 connector interfaces, especially where imported vehicles, European-style AC Type 2 interfaces, CCS arrangements, or tender specifications require IEC compatibility. A China domestic GB/T 20234 connector set can be technically sound for China vehicles but may not physically or electrically match the connector expectations of the Kazakhstan fleet or project.
target_standards: IEC 62196 series
IEC 61851 series
Kazakhstan project, vehicle-fleet, tender, or charging-network connector specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, and GB/T 20234.3-2023 for AC and DC conductive charging connection sets, together with GB/T 18487.1-2023 and GB/T 27930-2023 for charging behavior and DC communication. These documents should be treated as China-interface evidence unless the Kazakhstan buyer explicitly accepts GB/T connectors or requires multi-standard charger heads.
rows.gap: Confirm connector standard, inlet population, AC versus DC mix, communication protocol, cable temperature monitoring, locking method, adapter policy, and spare-part availability for the Kazakhstan fleet before finalizing the charger SKU.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific unless IEC 62196 or a connector type is mandated by tender, procurement, utility, charging-network, vehicle-fleet, or local installation specification
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, and GB/T 20234.3-2023 are the common China connector baseline for China-market conductive charging equipment and may be mandatory or procurement-specific under the relevant Chinese route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A GB/T 20234 connector package should not be assumed compatible with Kazakhstan projects that specify IEC 62196, Type 2, CCS, or fleet-specific interfaces.

rows.requirement: EAEU TR CU 020/2011 EMC for EV Chargers
rows.target_requirement: EV chargers with power electronics, controls, communication modules, contactors, metering, and network interfaces can fall within TR CU 020/2011 electromagnetic compatibility review. The Kazakhstan route should confirm emissions, immunity, surge, conducted disturbance, radiated disturbance, harmonic, flicker, and installation assumptions for the final charger cabinet and cable set.
target_standards: TR CU 020/2011 On electromagnetic compatibility of technical devices
Applicable GOST or ST RK EMC standards harmonized with IEC / CISPR references
EAEU conformity assessment and EAC marking rules
rows.china_equivalent: China dossiers may include GB/T 18487.1-2023 EMC clauses, GB/T 17626 series immunity tests, GB/T 9254 / CISPR-type emission reports where used, and charger-level EMC reports. These should be checked against the EAEU route, accepted standards list, operating voltage, cable length, enclosure, and communication options.
rows.gap: Map Chinese EMC reports to EAEU accepted clauses and retest if the final Kazakhstan SKU differs in cable harness, enclosure, power module, modem, payment terminal, RFID reader, or software-controlled operating mode.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the charger is within TR CU 020/2011 scope and is placed on the Kazakhstan / EAEU market
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or procurement-specific depending on Chinese product category, CCC / CQC / GB scope, and project requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EMC reports from China are not automatically sufficient for Kazakhstan. Confirm the EAEU TR CU 020/2011 route and retest configuration-sensitive charger variants where needed.

rows.requirement: EAEU TR CU 004/2011 Low-Voltage Safety for EV Chargers
rows.target_requirement: Kazakhstan participates in the Eurasian Economic Union conformity system, so EV charging equipment placed on the Kazakhstan market may need EAC conformity assessment where it falls within TR CU 004/2011 for low-voltage equipment. The dossier should address electrical shock protection, insulation, overheating, mechanical hazards, marking, instructions, and conformity documentation for the exact AC or DC charger configuration.
target_standards: TR CU 004/2011 On safety of low-voltage equipment
EAEU conformity assessment and EAC marking rules
Kazakhstan import and market-surveillance requirements for products in EAEU technical-regulation scope
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese EV charger safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 18487.1-2023, GB/T 18487.2 where applicable, GB/T 27930-2023 for DC communication where applicable, factory safety tests, CB or IEC reports, and component certificates. This evidence can support the EAC dossier but is not an EAC certificate or declaration by itself.
rows.gap: Confirm product scope, voltage rating, charger type, and EAC route before shipment. Prepare Russian-language and, where required, Kazakh-language labels and manuals, safety risk analysis, bill of materials, test reports, certificate or declaration route documents, and importer authorization.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the charger is within TR CU 004/2011 scope and is placed on the Kazakhstan / EAEU market
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or procurement-specific depending on Chinese product category, charger configuration, and project route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T safety evidence supports technical review, but Kazakhstan market placement may require an EAEU low-voltage conformity route and EAC marking where TR CU 004/2011 applies.

rows.requirement: GOST / ST RK and IEC 61851 Charger System Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Kazakhstan projects may specify GOST, ST RK, or IEC-family evidence for EV conductive charging systems, including IEC 61851 requirements for general system safety and charging modes. Even when EAC low-voltage and EMC conformity is handled separately, buyers, utilities, tenders, or engineers may request a clause-by-clause IEC 61851 package for AC and DC charger functionality.
target_standards: IEC 61851 series
Applicable GOST standards adopted from IEC 61851 where specified
Applicable ST RK standards adopted from IEC 61851 where specified
Kazakhstan project, tender, utility, or engineering specifications
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 18487.1-2023 is China's common conductive charging system baseline. It should be crosswalked against IEC 61851 mode definitions, control pilot behavior, protection functions, environmental ratings, emergency stop, insulation monitoring, and DC charger communication expectations.
rows.gap: Prepare a GB/T 18487-to-IEC 61851 crosswalk, identify any charger modes or protection features that differ from the Kazakhstan project specification, and provide test evidence for the exact AC wallbox, AC pedestal, DC fast charger, or cabinet variant.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where referenced by EAEU conformity route, Kazakhstan law, GOST / ST RK adoption, tender, utility, procurement, or project specification; otherwise commonly requested as technical evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 evidence is mandatory or procurement-specific depending on Chinese charger category, customer, and project route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 and IEC 61851 are related but not interchangeable without a documented crosswalk for the charger type, mode, protection logic, and project specification.

rows.requirement: KEGOC and Kazakhstan Grid Interface for High-Power Charging
rows.target_requirement: High-power charging hubs can become a distribution or transmission planning issue in Kazakhstan. KEGOC is the national electricity grid operator and system operator, while the practical connection route may involve a local distribution company, project owner, municipality, or industrial-site network. Review may focus on connection capacity, transformer loading, protection, metering, power quality, reactive power, demand management, telemetry, and commissioning.
target_standards: Kazakhstan electricity grid connection rules and technical conditions
KEGOC system-operator requirements where applicable
Local distribution utility technical specifications
Project-specific power-quality, metering, protection, and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China grid studies, load profiles, harmonic reports, charger power-module data, transformer sizing, and protection designs can support Kazakhstan review. They do not replace local technical conditions, available capacity checks, or utility approval for the actual site.
rows.gap: Prepare Kazakhstan site-load forecasts, simultaneity assumptions, harmonic and flicker calculations, transformer and feeder studies, protection coordination, earthing plan, metering design, communication interfaces, emergency shutdown logic, and commissioning procedures.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected charging sites requiring technical conditions, distribution connection, transmission interface, metering approval, or commissioning approval; project-specific for small private chargers
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese grid connection, charger capacity, site category, and local utility requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Product certificates do not approve a Kazakhstan high-power charging site. Local grid capacity, power quality, protection, metering, and commissioning acceptance must be handled separately.

rows.requirement: Kazakhstan EV Policy, Incentives, and Public Charging Projects
rows.target_requirement: Kazakhstan EV charger deployment can be shaped by national industrial policy, customs and tax treatment, municipal charging plans, public procurement, parking rules, tariff structures, and project subsidies or incentives. These policy measures do not replace product conformity assessment, but they can determine charger location, ownership model, payment function, data reporting, local-language documentation, and maintenance obligations.
target_standards: Kazakhstan national EV and transport policy measures
Kazakhstan customs and tax measures for electric vehicles and related equipment where applicable
Municipal charging-infrastructure plans and public procurement specifications
Project-specific tariff, payment, data, and maintenance requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China-side EV charger evidence may include GB/T reports, CQC or other voluntary certificates, OCPP or platform evidence, payment terminal documents, cybersecurity descriptions, and operating records. These materials should be adapted to Kazakhstan procurement, language, tax, payment, and service requirements.
china_standards: China charger platform, metering, and payment documentation
rows.gap: Before bidding or import, confirm whether the charger is for private, fleet, public, highway, municipal, or industrial use. Check procurement language, tax and customs codes, metering and payment rules, local service requirements, warranty obligations, and any public-network data interface.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; mandatory where a Kazakhstan law, customs measure, tax rule, public procurement, municipal permit, subsidy, tariff, or public-network requirement applies
mandatory_status.cn: Project-specific depending on Chinese export, customs, platform, payment, and customer requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Kazakhstan EV policy can affect charger deployment and procurement, but it does not replace EAEU conformity, connector compatibility, grid approval, or site permitting.

source.publisher: Adilet Legal Information System of Regulatory Legal Acts of the Republic of Kazakhstan
